subject: Ecology
title: The Urban Heat Island
description: A quick win: understand The Urban Heat Island.

hook.text: Cities are hotter than the countryside around them—sometimes by 5°C or more.

buildup.text: Dark roads and buildings absorb solar energy and radiate it as heat long after sunset.

discovery.text: Lack of vegetation means less evaporative cooling. Cars and AC units add waste heat.

twist.text: Heat islands kill more people than hurricanes, floods, and tornadoes combined in some years.

climax.text: Planting trees and using reflective surfaces can drop urban temperatures significantly.

punchline.text: Cities cook themselves. Trees cool them down.

quiz.question: What causes the urban heat island effect?
options: Dark surfaces absorb heat and vegetation is scarce
Cities are closer to the sun
